Non-Helpful: Moderated comments highlight past failures needing control.

Premise: Artificial Intelligence can play a game and not otherwise interact.
Claim: Machines are not gaining the upper hand on humans
Helpful: Limited interaction shows machine capabilities remain narrow.
Non-Helpful: Game mastery demonstrates superior machine strategic prowess.

Premise: Opioid training will help stop lazy doctors from over prescribing for pain.
Helpful: Training prevents overprescribing so must be required.
Non-Helpful: Training helps but requiring it infringes on professional autonomy.

Premise: Addiction is a result of a personality trait and the symptoms of that trait are drug abuse.
Claim: Marijuana is not a gateway drug
Helpful: Addiction stems from personality traits causing drug abuse.
Non-Helpful: Gateway drugs lead to abuse from addictive traits.

Premise: Comments, with good moderation, are a part of democracy.
Helpful: Good moderation enables democratic comment sections to function.
Non-Helpful: Poor moderation can cause democratic comment sections to fail.

Premise: College students constant questioning of everything lead them to not be swayed by lies and promises.
Claim: College students' votes do matter in an election
Helpful: Critical thinking leads to informed voting, influencing electoral outcomes.
Non-Helpful: Skepticism causes disengagement, making votes irrelevant.
